content.content:
- content: 'Internal note for claim appeal preparation: when a member has a denied
    claim and the

    remaining route is a formal appeal, prepare only the minimum packet needed to
    help the

    member organize the appeal. The initial provisional skeleton should include the
    claim ID,

    EOB or denial notice ID, service date, provider name, denial basis, and selected
    review

    process. A member-authored statement and the final attachment list may be marked
    pending

    and added later; they do not block process selection or preparation of the skeleton.


    Do not invent a member statement. Do not include payment information, appointment
    changes,

    portal passwords, verification codes, unrelated complete medical records, or a
    new prior

    authorization submission. Preparing the skeleton is not the same as filing the
    appeal.

    External filing requires separate explicit authorization.

    '
  metadata: {}
  rank: 1
  title: Internal Claim Appeal Packet Boundary Note
  url: null
- content: 'Internal note: when a bill is linked to a denied insurance claim that
    is still within an

    appeal or dispute period, do not treat payment as the first administrative resolution
    unless

    the user specifically asks to pay. The claim process should be resolved separately
    from

    appointment management or payment-plan setup.

    '
  metadata: {}
  rank: 2
  title: Internal Billing Hold Context for Claim Disputes
  url: null

content.content:
content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: Provider Reconsiderations and\
  \ Disputes | Denver Health Medical Plan\n- url: https://www.denverhealthmedicalplan.org/provider-reconsiderations-and-disputes-provider-complaintsappeals\n\
  - source: Denver Health Medical Plan\n- domain: denverhealthmedicalplan.org\n\n\
  ## Content\n\n# Provider Reconsiderations and Disputes\n\nDenver Health Medical\
  \ Plan describes provider reconsideration and dispute processes for claim-payment\
  \ disagreements. This page is directed to providers, not to members filing member\
  \ appeals. It addresses how DHMP reviews provider requests when the provider believes\
  \ a claim did not pay correctly.\n\nFor provider reconsideration, the page states\
  \ that the provider has sixty business days from the date of the original remittance\
  \ advice to request reconsideration. DHMP says it will make every effort to respond\
  \ within thirty business days. A provider reconsideration must be submitted with\
  \ the completed Provider Request for Payment Reconsideration form; otherwise, the\
  \ request is returned. DHMP uses the form to route the request to the correct department.\n\
  \nDHMP reviews the reconsideration request to determine whether the claim was submitted\
  \ timely, whether authorization and documentation guidelines were followed, and\
  \ whether proper reimbursement was applied. If the request arrives outside the reconsideration\
  \ timely filing period, DHMP says it will not be considered for review. The remittance\
  \ advice then reflects a timely-filing denial for reconsideration.\n\nIf the requirements\
  \ were met and the claim did not pay correctly, DHMP may overturn the original decision\
  \ and reprocess the claim for payment according to the provider agreement. If the\
  \ requirements were not met, DHMP maintains the original decision and directs the\
  \ provider to the dispute instructions for next steps.\n\nIf the provider does not\
  \ agree with the response to the reconsideration request, the dispute process is\
  \ the final step in DHMP\u2019s internal provider review process for a claims decision.\
  \ The provider has thirty calendar days from the date on the reconsideration remittance\
  \ advice to submit the Provider Request for Dispute Resolution form and supporting\
  \ documents.\n\nThe page explains different review timeframes for participating\
  \ and non-participating providers. Participating provider disputes are reviewed\
  \ within forty-five calendar days; non-participating provider disputes are reviewed\
  \ within ninety calendar days. A dispute must include the completed Provider Request\
  \ for Dispute Resolution form or it will be returned. The page also notes that clinical\
  \ review is not performed on claims payment disputes.\n\nThe reconsideration and\
  \ dispute forms described on this page are provider forms. Member appeals follow\
  \ separate plan appeal instructions and deadlines.\n"
metadata: {}
title: Provider Reconsiderations and Disputes | Denver Health Medical Plan
url: https://www.denverhealthmedicalplan.org/provider-reconsiderations-and-disputes-provider-complaintsappeals

content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: Prior Authorizations | Denver\
  \ Health Medical Plan\n- url: https://www.denverhealthmedicalplan.org/for-providers/prior-authorizations\n\
  - source: Denver Health Medical Plan\n- domain: denverhealthmedicalplan.org\n\n\
  ## Content\n\n# Prior Authorizations\n\nDenver Health Medical Plan explains how\
  \ providers request prior authorization for medical and behavioral health care.\
  \ Online prior authorization requests are available through the secure Provider\
  \ Portal. Providers without portal access should work with their organization\u2019\
  s local administrator or follow DHMP\u2019s provider portal registration process.\
  \ Faxed requests are also accepted for outpatient services, inpatient admit and\
  \ discharge notification, inpatient clinical records, and urgent or expedited requests.\n\
  \nMembers are directed to contact their physician to start an authorization. Providers\
  \ contact Utilization Management and submit authorization requests and supporting\
  \ clinical information. DHMP lists separate fax numbers for outpatient requests,\
  \ inpatient admit and discharge notifications, inpatient clinical records, and urgent\
  \ or expedited requests. Health Plan Services can answer questions about prior authorization.\n\
  \nDHMP\u2019s general rules distinguish prospective authorization, retrospective\
  \ requests, claims, and appeals. Some provider tiers and service categories require\
  \ prior authorization. Services must also be medically necessary and covered benefits.\
  \ Prior authorization is not a guarantee of payment because payment still depends\
  \ on eligibility, benefits, exclusions, medical necessity during review, and provider\
  \ status when services are rendered.\n\nDHMP states that urgent, emergency, and\
  \ observation care do not require prior authorization. It also states that services\
  \ submitted after the start of care are treated as retrospective, post-service requests.\
  \ Retrospective authorization requests can be reviewed for medical necessity when\
  \ they are within the post-service timeframe. If the request is more than fifteen\
  \ calendar days after service, the provider must add a rationale for late submission,\
  \ such as inability to verify insurance.\n\nRetrospective authorization requests\
  \ are not processed and are returned with instructions to follow the Claims Reconsideration\
  \ Process when claims have already been submitted or when the request is more than\
  \ ninety days after service. If a claim was already submitted, providers should\
  \ follow the Claims Department instructions for denials received because DHMP prior\
  \ authorization was not obtained before services were rendered.\n\nFor denials,\
  \ DHMP describes several possible next steps. A peer-to-peer review may be available\
  \ in some authorization denial situations, but it must occur between the ordering\
  \ provider, or an equally licensed alternative provider, and the physician reviewer.\
  \ Office personnel who are not licensed to write an order cannot conduct that call.\
  \ If required outreach attempts or the allowed period are missed, the next route\
  \ may be an appeal.\n\nFiling an appeal is described as a formal review process\
  \ governed by applicable state or national rules. Denial letters state the relevant\
  \ deadlines and requirements. If a deadline is missed, appeal rights may no longer\
  \ be available. The rules for who can represent a member depend on the plan line\
  \ of business. Providers can represent Medicare members without an Appointment of\
  \ Representative form, but non-Medicare plans require a signed representative form\
  \ from both the member and representative before an appeal can be processed.\n"
metadata: {}
title: Prior Authorizations | Denver Health Medical Plan
url: https://www.denverhealthmedicalplan.org/for-providers/prior-authorizations

content.content: I need user clarification or authorization before continuing, so I will stop here until the user responds.

Based on your records, this is the situation:

- **Service:** Outpatient lumbar-spine MRI on January 10, 2026  
- **Claim:** `CLM-DHMP-77821`  
- **Denial:** Prior authorization was required but missing  
- **EOB:** Final denial dated April 15, 2026 (`EOB-DHMP-44019`)  
- **Provider:** Rocky Mountain Diagnostic Imaging  
- **Provider reconsideration:** Not submitted  
- **Member appeal:** Not started; deadline is not recorded  

## Which process likely applies?

### 1. Ask the imaging provider to pursue reconsideration immediately
Denver Health Medical Plan’s provider instructions say reconsideration generally must be filed within **60 business days of the original remittance advice**. The record shows **63 business days have elapsed**, so the ordinary window may have passed—but the provider should still contact DHMP and ask whether a late reconsideration can be accepted or whether a dispute/exception is available.

Ask Rocky Mountain Diagnostic Imaging to:

- Review the denial and claim.
- Contact DHMP Claims or Utilization Management.
- Submit the appropriate **Provider Request for Payment Reconsideration**, if still accepted.
- Include the MRI order, clinical notes, referral/order information, and an explanation for why authorization was not obtained.
- Ask DHMP whether a **peer-to-peer review** or other provider review is available.

Do **not** rely on submitting a new prior-authorization request now. DHMP says post-service requests may be returned when the claim has already been submitted or when more than 90 days have passed since service.

### 2. Check the EOB for your member-appeal deadline
Because the provider window may be expired, your separate route may be a **formal member internal appeal**. The denial letter—not the claim record—controls the deadline and filing instructions, and that information is currently missing.

Look for wording such as:

- “Your right to appeal”
- “How to file an internal appeal”
- “Appeal must be filed within ___ days”
- “Where to send the appeal”
- “Expedited appeal” or “standard appeal”

Since this was a completed, non-urgent MRI, an expedited appeal generally would not apply unless there is an ongoing urgent medical issue.

### 3. If you file a member appeal
Explain that:

- The MRI was ordered and medically necessary.
- You reasonably expected the provider to handle any required authorization.
- You are asking DHMP to review the denial and not hold you responsible because the authorization process was not completed by the provider.
- The provider is being asked to submit records and reconsideration materials.

Attach the EOB, MRI order/referral, relevant clinical notes, and any provider correspondence. Avoid sending unrelated complete medical records.

### 4. Watch billing
If you receive a bill, contact the provider’s billing office and ask them to place the balance on administrative hold while the claim review or appeal is pending. Don’t pay solely because the claim was denied unless you decide to do so after reviewing your rights and the bill.

The most important next action is to **obtain the appeal deadline from the April 15 EOB and contact the provider today about reconsideration**.

Based on your records, this is the situation:

- **Service:** Outpatient lumbar-spine MRI on January 10, 2026  
- **Claim:** `CLM-DHMP-77821`  
- **Denial:** Prior authorization was required but missing  
- **EOB:** Final denial dated April 15, 2026 (`EOB-DHMP-44019`)  
- **Provider:** Rocky Mountain Diagnostic Imaging  
- **Provider reconsideration:** Not submitted  
- **Member appeal:** Not started; deadline is not recorded  

## Which process likely applies?

### 1. Ask the imaging provider to pursue reconsideration immediately
Denver Health Medical Plan’s provider instructions say reconsideration generally must be filed within **60 business days of the original remittance advice**. The record shows **63 business days have elapsed**, so the ordinary window may have passed—but the provider should still contact DHMP and ask whether a late reconsideration can be accepted or whether a dispute/exception is available.

Ask Rocky Mountain Diagnostic Imaging to:

- Review the denial and claim.
- Contact DHMP Claims or Utilization Management.
- Submit the appropriate **Provider Request for Payment Reconsideration**, if still accepted.
- Include the MRI order, clinical notes, referral/order information, and an explanation for why authorization was not obtained.
- Ask DHMP whether a **peer-to-peer review** or other provider review is available.

Do **not** rely on submitting a new prior-authorization request now. DHMP says post-service requests may be returned when the claim has already been submitted or when more than 90 days have passed since service.

### 2. Check the EOB for your member-appeal deadline
Because the provider window may be expired, your separate route may be a **formal member internal appeal**. The denial letter—not the claim record—controls the deadline and filing instructions, and that information is currently missing.

Look for wording such as:

- “Your right to appeal”
- “How to file an internal appeal”
- “Appeal must be filed within ___ days”
- “Where to send the appeal”
- “Expedited appeal” or “standard appeal”

Since this was a completed, non-urgent MRI, an expedited appeal generally would not apply unless there is an ongoing urgent medical issue.

### 3. If you file a member appeal
Explain that:

- The MRI was ordered and medically necessary.
- You reasonably expected the provider to handle any required authorization.
- You are asking DHMP to review the denial and not hold you responsible because the authorization process was not completed by the provider.
- The provider is being asked to submit records and reconsideration materials.

Attach the EOB, MRI order/referral, relevant clinical notes, and any provider correspondence. Avoid sending unrelated complete medical records.

### 4. Watch billing
If you receive a bill, contact the provider’s billing office and ask them to place the balance on administrative hold while the claim review or appeal is pending. Don’t pay solely because the claim was denied unless you decide to do so after reviewing your rights and the bill.

The most important next action is to **obtain the appeal deadline from the April 15 EOB and contact the provider today about reconsideration**.

text.regex:
(?isx)
\A
(?:
  .* </think> \s* (?! .* </think> )
  |
  (?! .* </think> )
)
# Path B must be selected decisively, not merely listed as one option.
(?=
  .*?
  (?:
    (?:file|submit|start|pursue|proceed[\s_-]*with|use|choose|prioritize|recommend(?:ed)?|next[\s_-]*step(?:[\s_-]*is)?|current[\s_-]*(?:route|path|option)|appropriate[\s_-]*(?:route|path|option))
    [^.!?\n]{0,120}
    (?:
      (?:member[\s_-]*)?(?:internal|formal|coverage|plan)[\s_-]*appeal
      |member[\s_-]*appeal
      |appeal[^.!?\n]{0,60}(?:with|to)[^.!?\n]{0,50}(?:dhmp|denver[\s_-]*health[\s_-]*medical[\s_-]*plan|insurer|health[\s_-]*plan)
    )
    |
    (?:member[\s_-]*)?(?:internal|formal|coverage|plan)[\s_-]*appeal
    [^.!?\n]{0,120}
    (?:is|remains|should[\s_-]*be|is[\s_-]*the|as[\s_-]*the)?[\s_-]*
    (?:available|open|timely|appropriate|correct|recommended|primary|current|next|remaining|the[\s_-]*route|the[\s_-]*path)
  )
)
# Path A must be closed by the 60-business-day boundary and no filing.
(?=
  .*?
  (?:
    (?:provider(?:['’]s)?[\s_-]*(?:claims?[\s_-]*)?|claims?[\s_-]*|payment[\s_-]*)
    (?:reconsideration|review|dispute)
    [^.!?\n]{0,180}
    (?:
      (?:is|has[\s_-]*become|remains)[\s_-]*(?:expired|closed|unavailable|untimely|late|outside[\s_-]*(?:the[\s_-]*)?(?:filing[\s_-]*)?window)
      |(?:is|has)[\s_-]*no[\s_-]*longer[\s_-]*(?:available|open|timely|applicable)
      |[^.!?\n]{0,40}(?:no[\s_-]*longer[\s_-]*(?:available|open|timely|applicable)|not[\s_-]*(?:available|open|timely|applicable)|expired|closed|untimely)
      |(?:deadline|filing[\s_-]*window|time[\s_-]*limit)[^.!?\n]{0,80}(?:has[\s_-]*)?(?:passed|expired|closed)
      |(?:63|sixty[\s_-]*three)[\s_-]*business[\s_-]*days[^.!?\n]{0,80}(?:60|sixty)[\s_-]*business[\s_-]*days
    )
    |
    (?:expired|closed|unavailable|untimely|outside[\s_-]*(?:the[\s_-]*)?(?:filing[\s_-]*)?window|no[\s_-]*longer[\s_-]*(?:available|open|timely|applicable))
    [^.!?\n]{0,120}
    (?:provider(?:['’]s)?[\s_-]*(?:claims?[\s_-]*)?|claims?[\s_-]*|payment[\s_-]*)
    (?:reconsideration|review|dispute)
  )
)
# Path C must be kept out unless the prerequisite/urgent exception arises.
(?=
  .*?
  (?:
    (?:external|independent)[\s_-]*(?:review|appeal)
    [^.!?\n]{0,180}
    (?:
      is[\s_-]*not[\s_-]*yet[\s_-]*(?:available|applicable|appropriate|the[\s_-]*next[\s_-]*step)
      |is[\s_-]*not[\s_-]*(?:currently[\s_-]*)?(?:available|applicable|appropriate|open)
      |[^.!?\n]{0,40}(?:not[\s_-]*yet[\s_-]*(?:available|applicable|appropriate)|not[\s_-]*(?:currently[\s_-]*)?(?:available|applicable|appropriate|open))
      |(?:comes?|follows?|is[\s_-]*available)[\s_-]*(?:only[\s_-]*)?after[\s_-]*(?:a|the)?[\s_-]*internal[\s_-]*appeal
      |requires?[\s_-]*(?:a|the)?[\s_-]*(?:final[\s_-]*)?internal[\s_-]*(?:appeal[\s_-]*)?denial
      |(?:may|can)[\s_-]*be[\s_-]*(?:concurrent|simultaneous)[^.!?\n]{0,80}urgent
      |unless[^.!?\n]{0,80}urgent
    )
    |
    (?:after[\s_-]*(?:a|the)?[\s_-]*internal[\s_-]*appeal|unless[^.!?\n]{0,80}urgent)
    [^.!?\n]{0,120}
    (?:external|independent)[\s_-]*(?:review|appeal)
  )
)
# Contradictory or explicitly unresolved answers must not pass merely
# because they mention all three concepts somewhere in the response.
(?!
  .*?
  (?<!not[\s_-])
  (?<!do[\s_-])
  (?<!don't[\s_-])
  (?:contact|ask|have|tell|instruct|work[\s_-]*with)
  [^.!?\n]{0,100}
  (?:provider|imaging[\s_-]*center|billing|physician)
  [^.!?\n]{0,120}
  (?:submit|file|request|start|pursue)
  [^.!?\n]{0,80}
  (?:reconsideration|provider[\s_-]*dispute|claims?[\s_-]*review)
)
(?!
  .*?
  (?:provider(?:['’]s)?[\s_-]*(?:claims?[\s_-]*)?|claims?[\s_-]*|payment[\s_-]*)
  (?:reconsideration|review|dispute)
  [^.!?\n]{0,100}
  (?:may|might|possibly|perhaps|appears?|seems?)
  [^.!?\n]{0,80}
  (?:expired|closed|unavailable|untimely|late|outside)
)
(?!
  .*?
  (?:not[\s_-]*sure|uncertain|cannot[\s_-]*determine|need(?:s)?[\s_-]*to[\s_-]*confirm)
  [^.!?\n]{0,120}
  (?:whether|if)?
  [^.!?\n]{0,80}
  (?:file|submit|start|pursue|choose|use)?
  [^.!?\n]{0,80}
  (?:member[\s_-]*)?(?:internal|formal|coverage|plan)?[\s_-]*appeal
)
.*\Z
